title: Phleum abbreviatum
intro: Phleum abbreviatum, also known as short meadow-grass or short timothy grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia, often used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Phleum abbreviatum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Phleum abbreviatum is commonly used in herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, and is also used in the production of traditional folk remedies for respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Phleum abbreviatum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Phleum, which includes several species of meadow grasses.
